protocol.name: wlr_gamma_control_unstable_v1

interface.name: zwlr_gamma_control_manager_v1

description.summary: manager to create per-output gamma controls

interface.description: This interface is a manager that allows creating per-output gamma controls.

request.name: get_gamma_control

description.summary: get a gamma control for an output

request.description: Create a gamma control that can be used to adjust gamma tables for the provided output.

request.name: destroy
request.description: All objects created by the manager will still remain valid, until their appropriate destroy request has been called.

interface.name: zwlr_gamma_control_v1

description.summary: adjust gamma tables for an output

interface.description:
This interface allows a client to adjust gamma tables for a particular output.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is experimental and backward incompatible changes may be made. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes are done by bumping the version number in the protocol and interface names and resetting the interface version. Once the protocol is to be declared stable, the 'z' prefix and the version number in the protocol and interface names are removed and the interface version number is reset.

event.name: gamma_size
event.description:
Advertise the size of each gamma ramp.
This event is sent immediately when the gamma control object is created.

arg.name: size
arg.summary: number of elements in a ramp

request.name: set_gamma

description.summary: set the gamma tables for an output

request.description:
Set the gamma table. The file descriptor can be memory-mapped to provide the raw gamma table, which contains successive gamma ramps for the red, green and blue channels. Each gamma ramp is an array of 16- bit unsigned integer values, with the most significant byte first.
The file descriptor data must have the same length as three times the ramp size, multiplied by the size of a single value (2 bytes).

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event indicates that the gamma control is no longer valid. This can happen for a number of reasons, including: - The output doesn't support gamma tables - Setting the gamma tables failed - Another client already has exclusive gamma control for this output - The compositor has reset the gamma tables to an unspecified value
The client should destroy this object in response to this event.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the gamma control object. If the object is still valid, this restores the original gamma tables of the output.
